top_findings.summary: The skill description states it's for "compliance checking," "monitoring," and "validation," but requests unlimited Bash execution (`Bash(*)`) alongside comprehensive write/edit permissions, which exceeds the read-only nature of compliance assessment. Compliance checking typically requires read access to inspect configurations, not the ability to execute arbitrary system commands and make structural changes. The prerequisites also call for "making structural changes" and production deployment capabilities, transforming what should be an auditing tool into a system modification tool with excessive privileges beyond its stated purpose.
